shared.reviewNotice: This page is a launch-safe draft and must be reviewed by qualified legal counsel before the public production launch.

metadata.title: Privacy Policy
metadata.description: Draft privacy policy for elReda Advertising, covering contact data, quote requests, cookies, and legal review status.

privacyPolicy.title: Privacy Policy
privacyPolicy.description: A plain-language draft explaining what information may be collected when you use the elReda Advertising website.

sections.title: Information we may collect
body: When you contact us or request a quote, we may receive the information you choose to provide, such as your name, company, email address, phone number, project details, service interests, budget range, and message content.
The website may also process basic technical information needed to operate the site, protect forms, measure performance, and understand consent preferences.

sections.title: How information may be used
body: We use submitted information to respond to inquiries, prepare project discussions, manage quote requests, improve the website experience, and maintain reasonable operational records.
We do not use this draft page to make final legal commitments. Final processing terms, retention periods, and user rights must be confirmed by legal review.

sections.title: Service providers
body: The website may use trusted service providers for hosting, content management, form storage, consent management, analytics after consent, error monitoring, and file handling where enabled.
Production configuration and final provider terms must be verified before launch.

sections.title: Your choices
body: You can contact us at info@elreda.com about privacy questions or requests related to information you submitted through the website.
Cookie preferences are managed through the Cookie Preferences control in the footer when the production CookieYes key is configured.

metadata.title: Terms
metadata.description: Draft website terms for elReda Advertising, covering website use, inquiries, project discussions, and legal review status.

terms.title: Terms
terms.description: A launch-safe draft of basic website terms for visitors and prospective clients.

sections.title: Website use
body: This website presents information about elReda Advertising services, work approach, and ways to contact the team.
You should not misuse the website, attempt unauthorized access, submit harmful content, or interfere with normal operation.

sections.title: Service inquiries
body: Submitting a contact form, quote request, or message does not create a client relationship, contract, or obligation to start work.
Project scope, fees, timelines, deliverables, payment terms, and ownership terms must be agreed separately in a written proposal or contract.

sections.title: Content and accuracy
body: We aim to keep website information clear and useful, but service descriptions, examples, timelines, and availability may change.
Final commercial commitments should be confirmed directly with the elReda Advertising team.

sections.title: Legal review required
body: These terms are draft website copy for launch readiness and are not a substitute for final legal terms prepared for the business.
Qualified legal counsel should review and approve this page before the public production launch.

metadata.title: Cookies Policy
metadata.description: Draft cookies policy for elReda Advertising, covering essential cookies, consent preferences, and analytics after consent.

cookiesPolicy.title: Cookies Policy
cookiesPolicy.description: A draft explanation of how cookies and similar technologies may be used on the website.

sections.title: What cookies are
body: Cookies are small files or identifiers stored by a browser to help a website work, remember choices, or support measurement and security features.
Similar technologies may also be used for consent management, analytics, error monitoring, or embedded services.

sections.title: Types of cookies
body: Essential cookies may be used to operate the website, preserve language or consent choices, and support security or form behavior.
Analytics or marketing cookies should only run according to the configured consent settings and production tag setup.

sections.title: Managing preferences
body: When the production CookieYes key is configured, visitors can use the Cookie Preferences control in the footer to review or change consent choices.
Browser settings may also allow you to block or delete cookies, but some website features may not work as expected.

sections.title: Production verification
body: Cookie categories, consent mode, analytics tags, and retention details must be verified with the final CookieYes production configuration before launch.
This draft should be reviewed by legal counsel before publication.

metadata.title: Accessibility
metadata.description: Draft accessibility statement for elReda Advertising, covering bilingual accessibility intent and feedback contact.

accessibility.title: Accessibility Statement
accessibility.description: A draft statement of elReda Advertising's intent to provide an accessible bilingual website experience.

sections.title: Our intent
body: elReda Advertising aims to provide a website experience that is usable by visitors across Arabic and English, keyboard navigation, and a range of assistive technologies.
The site is built with semantic landmarks, visible focus states, skip navigation, labeled form fields, and RTL/LTR support.

sections.title: Known review needs
body: Automated accessibility checks are part of the development workflow, but manual Arabic and English screen reader testing remains required before public production launch.
Real-device testing should also be completed with final content and production integrations.

sections.title: Feedback
body: If you find an accessibility issue, contact us at info@elreda.com and include the page URL, device, browser, and a description of the issue.
We will use feedback to prioritize improvements and reduce access barriers.

sections.title: Legal review required
body: This accessibility statement is a draft for route readiness. Any final compliance claims, standards references, or legal obligations should be reviewed before launch.
